a. m.


Two German classes of the Wom-
ans club held meeting today at
the Palace store. Mrs. Prayer, who
has so successfully conducted the
classes the past season, will re-
sumo her work next fall.


& & 4


Mrs. John G. Byrne has been
among the popular hostesses this
season. She will entertain the men,
bers of the Northwestern Univer.
sity club on Tuesday evening, May
81, at her home on Tenth avenue.


& = 4


Mrs. George Dana Linn has as
her guest her sister. Miss Elizabeth
Mckay of Pullman.


Miss L. Odell REESE of North Yak
ina and Edgar w. Smith of Mime
apolis were married Wednesday
evening at the Holy Trinity Episco-
pal church. The service was read
by the Rev. Mr.. Worthing. The
affair was very quiet and unpreten-
tious.


Following a wedding trip to the
coast, Mr.. and Mrs. Smith will make
their home in this city.


= & 4


The last meeting Of the parlia-
mentary law department of the
Woman's club was held yesterday
with Mrs. A. P. Fassett in the
library. In a review of the past
years work, Mrs. o. A. Thompson
and Mrs. w. M. Gleason made the
same score in correct answers, and
both received tokens of hand
painted china.


4 4 4


The ladies' aid of the Norwegian
Lutheran church met yesterday
afternoon with Mrs. F. A. Foss at
her home, 01413 Washington street.


4 & &


The Damrosch Symphony orches-
ira scored big success in the con-
cert given by them last night at the
Auditorium theater under the au-
spices of the Wagner club.


Each number was delightfully."-
gratifying and especially in the
Wagnerian numbers did the audi
ence laud the director and his Or
chestra. A feature of the evening
was the appearance of the Mendels-
sohn club. under the direction of
ii. w. Newton.


& 4 4


The WOman's alliance of the First
Unitarian church served the last
business mems luncheon of the sea.
son today in the basement of the
church.


& z 4


The officers of Spokane camp
No. 1862, Royal Neighbors, win
meet for practice tomorrow after.
noon at 1870 o'clock in upper Pa
cific halls.
